entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A dominant cereal crop in southern Africa used in sole-cropping and intercropping systems

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: A group of crops advocated to enhance productivity and soil fertility in resource-poor farming systems

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The broad geographic region where sole-cropped maize is the dominant cropping system

entities.name: MALAWI
entities.description: The specific country where farmer participatory research on legume intensification was conducted

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop yield and efficiency that legumes are intended to enhance

entities.name: FARMER PARTICIPATORY RESEARCH
entities.description: A research approach involving farmers in testing legume intensification to improve maize-based systems

entities.name: LEGUME INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: The practice of increasing legume use to improve the productivity and sustainability of maize-based systems

entities.name: MAIZE-BASED SYSTEMS
entities.description: Cropping systems centered around the production of maize

entities.name: GRAIN/LEGUME INTERCROPS
entities.description: A cropping method where grains and legumes, such as pigeonpea or groundnut, are grown together

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A long-duration legume (Cajanus cajan) used in intercropping systems

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume (Arachis hypogaea) used in intercropping systems

entities.name: RELAY GREEN MANURE SYSTEM
entities.description: A system where Tephrosia vogelii is used as green manure in conjunction with maize

entities.name: TEPHROSIA VOGELII
entities.description: A plant species used in relay green manure systems to improve soil

entities.name: ON-FARM EXPERIMENTATION
entities.description: The process of testing agricultural technologies under actual farming conditions

entities.name: RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant material left after harvest, which can contain nitrogen and improve soil

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A key nutrient found in legume residues that enhances soil fertility

entities.name: DRY LAKESHORE ECOLOGY
entities.description: A specific environmental condition in Malawi where legume-intensified systems were tested

entities.name: FARMER ASSESSMENT
entities.description: The process of farmers evaluating the performance and desirability of agricultural technologies

entities.name: ADOPTION
entities.description: The process by which farmers accept and integrate new agricultural technologies into their practices

entities.name: SURVEYS
entities.description: A data collection tool used to identify constraints and trade-offs underlying technology choice

entities.name: CONSTRAINTS AND TRADE-OFFS
entities.description: Factors and compromises that influence a farmer's decision to adopt a new technology

entities.name: MAIZE PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing and harvesting maize

entities.name: MARKET CONDITION
entities.description: Economic factors in the market that influence the choice of crops and technologies

entities.name: FARM RESOURCES
entities.description: The available assets and inputs on a farm that affect technology choice

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD COMPOSITION
entities.description: The structure and demographics of the farming household that influence labor and decision-making

entities.name: WEED SUPPRESSION
entities.description: A beneficial effect of legume intensification that reduces weed growth

entities.name: CASH EARNINGS
entities.description: Potential financial income generated from the production of legumes

entities.name: SEED ACCESS
entities.description: The ability of farmers to obtain the necessary seeds for planting

entities.name: LABOUR REQUIREMENTS
entities.description: The amount of human work required to maintain legume-intensified systems

entities.name: GRAIN MARKET ACCESS AND PRICE
entities.description: The availability of markets and the prevailing prices for grain crops

entities.name: BURNING
entities.description: A common practice used by farmers to manage crop residues

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients to plants, potentially improved by legumes

entities.name: MARKET RETURNS
entities.description: The financial profit derived from legume production
